heading: Tax on income of foreign corporations not connected with United States business
label: 881 Tax on income of foreign corporations not connected with United States business
notes: References in TextThe date of the enactment of this paragraph, referred to in subsec. (b)(2)(B), is the date of enactment of , which was approved .Pub. L. 108–357Oct. 22, 2004

L. 100–647“(ii) Paragraph (4) of section 954(b) (relating to corporations not formed or availed of to avoid tax).“(iii) Subparagraph (B) of section 954(c)(3) (relating to certain income derived in active conduct of trade or business).“(iv) Subparagraph (C) of section 954(c)(3) (relating to certain income derived by an insurance company).“(v) Subparagraphs (A) and (B) of section 954(c)(4) (relating to exception for certain income received from related persons).”1986—Subsec. (a)(3)(A). , amended subpar. (A) generally, striking out “any gain not in excess of” before “the original issue discount”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1810(e)(2)(B)Subsec. (a)(3)(B). , amended subpar. (B) generally. Prior to amendment, subpar. (B) read as follows: “the payment of interest on an original issue discount obligation, an amount equal to the original issue discount accrued on such obligation since the last payment of interest thereon (except that such original issue discount shall be taken into account under this subparagraph only to the extent that the tax thereon does not exceed the interest payment less the tax imposed by paragraph (1) thereon), and”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1810(e)(2)(B)Subsec. (a)(4). , struck out “or from payments which are treated as being so contingent under section 871(e),” after “sold or exchanged,”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1211(b)(6)Subsec. (b)(1). , amended par. (1) generally. Prior to amendment, par. (1) read as follows: “For purposes of this section, a corporation created or organized in Guam or the Virgin Islands or under the law of Guam or the Virgin Islands shall not be treated as a foreign corporation for any taxable year if—Pub. L. 99–514, § 1273(b)(1)“(A) at all times during such taxable year less than 25 percent in value of the stock of such corporation is owned (directly or indirectly) by foreign persons, and“(B) at least 20 percent of the gross income of such corporation is shown to the satisfaction of the Secretary to have been derived from sources within Guam or the Virgin Islands (as the case may be) for the 3-year period ending with the close of the preceding taxable year of such corporation (or for such part of such period as the corporation has been in existence).”Subsec. (b)(2). , (2)(A), redesignated par. (3) as (2) and struck out former par. (2) which provided that par. (1) of this subsection not apply with respect to income tax liability incurred to Guam.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1273(b)(1)Subsec. (b)(2)(A). , substituted “paragraph” for “Paragraph”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1899A(22)Subsec. (b)(3), (4). , redesignated par. (3) as (2) and struck out par. (4) which provided a cross reference to sections 934 and 934A.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1273(b)(2)(A)Subsec. (c). , made clarifying amendment to directory language of . See 1984 Amendment note below.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1899A(68)Pub. L. 98–369, § 127(b)(1)Subsec. (c)(2). , (3)(C), inserted “which would be subject to tax under subsection (a) but for this subsection and” in introductory provisions and substituted “receives a statement” for “has received a statement” in subpar. (B)(ii).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1810(d)(1)(B)Subsec. (c)(3)(C). , inserted a closing parenthesis following “section 864(d)(4)”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1899A(23)Subsec. (c)(4)(A)(i). , substituted “less than 5 percent or $1,000,000” for “less than 10 percent”.Pub. L. 99–514, § 1223(b)(2)Subsecs. (d), (e). , added subsec. (d) and redesignated former subsec. (d) as (e).Pub. L. 99–514, § 1214(c)(2)1984—Subsec. (a). , substituted “Except as provided in subsection (c), there” for “There” in introductory provision.Pub. L. 98–369, § 127(b)(2)Subsec. (a)(1). , substituted “section 1273” for “section 1232(b)”.Pub. L. 98–369, § 42(a)(10)Subsec. (a)(3). , amended par. (3) generally, substituting in subpar. (A), “a sale or exchange of an original issue discount obligation, the amount of any gain not in excess of the original issue discount accruing while such obligation was held by the foreign corporation (to the extent such discount was not theretofore taken into account under subparagraph (B)), and” for “bonds or other evidences of indebtedness issued after , and before , amounts which under section 1232(a)(2)(B) are considered as ordinary income, and, in the case of corporate obligations issued after , and before , amounts which would be so considered but for the fact the obligations were issued after ,”, substituting in subpar. (B), “the payment of interest on an original issue discount obligation, an amount equal to the original issue discount accrued on such obligation since the last payment of interest thereon (except that such original issue discount shall be taken into account under this subparagraph only to the extent that the tax thereon does not exceed the interest payment less the tax imposed by paragraph (1) thereon), and” for “bonds or other evidences of indebtedness issued after , and payable more than 6 months from the date of original issue (without regard to the period held by the taxpayer), amounts which under section 1232(a)(2)(B) would be considered as ordinary income but for the fact such obligations were issued after , and”, and striking out subpar. (C) which required that in the case of the payment of interest on an obligation described in subpar. (B), an amount equal to the original issue discount, but not in excess of such interest less the tax imposed by par. (1) thereon, accrued on such obligation since the last payment of interest thereon, be included for purpose of the 30 percent tax.Pub. (a). substantially revised the income tax treatment of foreign corporations, substituted the concept of amounts received from sources within the United States by foreign corporations but not effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business within the United States for the concept of amounts received from sources within the United States by foreign corporations not engaged in trade or business within the United States as the amount upon which the existing 30 percent levy should be imposed, and added contingent income received from the sale of patents and other intangibles and amounts of original issue discount which are treated as ordinary income received on retirement or sale or exchange of bonds or other evidences of indebtedness issued after , to the specified types of fixed or determinable income.Pub. children.text: interest (other than original issue discount as defined in section 1273), dividends, rents, salaries, wages, premiums, annuities, compensations, remunerations, emoluments, and other fixed or determinable annual or periodical gains, profits, and income,
gains described in section 631(b) or (c),

children.label: A
children.text: a sale or exchange of an original issue discount obligation, the amount of the original issue discount accruing while such obligation was held by the foreign corporation (to the extent such discount was not theretofore taken into account under subparagraph (B)), and

children.label: B
children.text: a payment on an original issue discount obligation, an amount equal to the original issue discount accruing while such obligation was held by the foreign corporation (except that such original issue discount shall be taken into account under this subparagraph only to the extent such discount was not theretofore taken into account under this subparagraph and only to the extent that the tax thereon does not exceed the payment less the tax imposed by paragraph (1) thereon), and

gains from the sale or exchange after , of patents, copyrights, secret processes and formulas, good will, trademarks, trade brands, franchises, and other like property, or of any interest in any such property, to the extent such gains are from payments which are contingent on the productivity, use, or disposition of the property or interest sold or exchanged,October 4, 1966

children.heading: Guam, American Samoa, the Northern Mariana Islands, and the Virgin Islands
children.label: 1 Guam, American Samoa, the Northern Mariana Islands, and the Virgin Islands
A
children.text: at all times during such taxable year less than 25 percent in value of the stock of such corporation is beneficially owned (directly or indirectly) by foreign persons,

children.label: B
children.text: at least 65 percent of the gross income of such corporation is shown to the satisfaction of the Secretary to be effectively connected with the conduct of a trade or business in such a possession or the United States for the 3-year period ending with the close of the taxable year of such corporation (or for such part of such period as the corporation or any predecessor has been in existence), and

children.label: C
children.text: no substantial part of the income of such corporation is used (directly or indirectly) to satisfy obligations to persons who are not bona fide residents of such a possession or the United States.

children.label: i
children.text: created or organized in, or under the law of, the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, and

children.label: ii
children.text: with respect to which the requirements of subparagraphs (A), (B), and (C) of paragraph (1) are met for the taxable year,

children.heading: Applicability
children.label: B Applicability
children.text: If, on or after the date of the enactment of this paragraph, an increase in the rate of the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico’s withholding tax which is generally applicable to dividends paid to United States corporations not engaged in a trade or business in the Commonwealth to a rate greater than 10 percent takes effect, this paragraph shall not apply to dividends received on or after the effective date of the increase.

children.label: i
children.text: a United States person, or

children.label: ii
children.text: a person who would be a United States person if references to the United States in section 7701 included references to a possession of the United States.

children.heading: Indirect ownership rules
children.label: B Indirect ownership rules
children.text: For purposes of paragraph (1), the rules of section 318(a)(2) shall apply except that “5 percent” shall be substituted for “50 percent” in subparagraph (C) thereof.

children.heading: Repeal of tax on interest of foreign corporations received from certain portfolio debt investments
children.label: c Repeal of tax on interest of foreign corporations received from certain portfolio debt investments

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In the case of any portfolio interest received by a foreign corporation from sources within the United States, no tax shall be imposed under paragraph (1) or (3) of subsection (a).

children.label: A
children.text: would be subject to tax under subsection (a) but for this subsection, and

children.label: i
children.text: which is in registered form, and

children.label: I
children.text: the person who would otherwise be required to deduct and withhold tax from such interest under section 1442(a) receives a statement which meets the requirements of section 871(h)(5) that the beneficial owner of the obligation is not a United States person, or

children.label: II
children.text: the Secretary has determined that such a statement is not required in order to carry out the purposes of this subsection.

children.heading: Portfolio interest shall not include interest received by certain persons
children.label: 3 Portfolio interest shall not include interest received by certain persons
A
children.text: except in the case of interest paid on an obligation of the United States, is received by a bank on an extension of credit made pursuant to a loan agreement entered into in the ordinary course of its trade or business,

children.label: B
children.text: is received by a 10-percent shareholder (within the meaning of section 871(h)(3)(B)), or

children.label: C
children.text: is received by a controlled foreign corporation from a related person (within the meaning of section 864(d)(4)).

children.heading: Portfolio interest not to include certain contingent interest
children.label: 4 Portfolio interest not to include certain contingent interest
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “portfolio interest” shall not include any interest which is treated as not being portfolio interest under the rules of section 871(h)(4).

children.heading: Special rules for controlled foreign corporations
children.label: 5 Special rules for controlled foreign corporations
i
children.text: Subparagraph (A) of section 954(b)(3) (relating to exception where foreign base company income is less than 5 percent or $1,000,000).

children.label: ii
children.text: Paragraph (4) of section 954(b) (relating to exception for certain income subject to high foreign taxes).

children.label: iii
children.text: Clause (i) of section 954(c)(3)(A) (relating to certain income received from related persons).

children.heading: Controlled foreign corporation
children.label: B Controlled foreign corporation
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “controlled foreign corporation” has the meaning given to such term by section 957(a).

children.heading: Secretary may cease application of this subsection
children.label: 6 Secretary may cease application of this subsection
children.text: Under rules similar to the rules of section 871(h)(6), the Secretary may provide that this subsection shall not apply to payments of interest described in section 871(h)(6).

children.heading: Registered form
children.label: 7 Registered form
children.text: For purposes of this subsection, the term “registered form” has the meaning given such term by section 163(f).

children.heading: Tax not to apply to certain interest and dividends
children.label: d Tax not to apply to certain interest and dividends
children.text: No tax shall be imposed under paragraph (1) or (3) of subsection (a) on any amount described in section 871(i)(2).

children.heading: Tax not to apply to certain dividends of regulated investment companies
children.label: e Tax not to apply to certain dividends of regulated investment companies

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Except as provided in subparagraph (B), no tax shall be imposed under paragraph (1) of subsection (a) on any interest-related dividend (as defined in section 871(k)(1)) received from a regulated investment company.

children.label: i
children.text: to any dividend referred to in section 871(k)(1)(B), and

children.label: ii
children.text: to any interest-related dividend received by a controlled foreign corporation (within the meaning of section 957(a)) to the extent such dividend is attributable to interest received by the regulated investment company from a person who is a related person (within the meaning of section 864(d)(4)) with respect to such controlled foreign corporation.

children.heading: Treatment of dividends received by controlled foreign corporations
children.label: C Treatment of dividends received by controlled foreign corporations
children.text: The rules of subsection (c)(5)(A) shall apply to any interest-related dividend received by a controlled foreign corporation (within the meaning of section 957(a)) to the extent such dividend is attributable to interest received by the regulated investment company which is described in clause (ii) of section 871(k)(1)(E) (and not described in clause (i) or (iii) of such section).

children.heading: Short-term capital gain dividends
children.label: 2 Short-term capital gain dividends
children.text: No tax shall be imposed under paragraph (1) of subsection (a) on any short-term capital gain dividend (as defined in section 871(k)(2)) received from a regulated investment company.

children.heading: Cross reference
children.label: f Cross reference
children.text: For doubling of tax on corporations of certain foreign countries, see section 891.For special rules for original issue discount, see section 871(g).
